I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

input.initialPrompt: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

data.reasoningText: The user wants to set up hot reload for their .NET test project so they can iterate faster on failing tests. Let me first look at the project files to understand the setup.

data.content: Starting `dotnet watch test` — this will re-run your tests automatically whenever you save a file:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. This is a .NET MSTest project. I can start `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically when files change.

Let me start it up.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-9333ced1708d4fa79f69861b06b1790c && dotnet watch test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content: The `--no-pager` flag caused an issue. Let me restart without it:
data.reasoningText: The `--no-pager` flag was passed through to MSBuild which doesn't recognize it. Let me restart without that flag.

`dotnet watch test` is now running. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — failed (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

The watcher is live and will **automatically re-run tests** whenever you save a file. The failing test expects express shipping to return `15.00` but `CalculateShipping` returns `10.00m` for `"express"`. Want me to fix it?
